common.saveFailed: Failed to save. Please try again.

pageStitch.title: Stitch (針法)
pageStitch.body: Choose the stitch pattern for this row.

pageTotalStitches.title: Total stitches (總針數)
pageTotalStitches.body: This column shows the current total stitch count.
pageTotalStitches.statusIntro: Use the color to quickly validate the stitch count:
pageTotalStitches.bullet1: It helps you validate sizing and shaping while editing.
pageTotalStitches.bullet2: If the total looks wrong, double-check the stitch settings in this row and previous rows.

items.openEditor: Tap any Stitch cell → opens the editor
items.changeOrAdd: Use the top tabs: Change stitch / Add to bundle

pageChangeStitch.title: Change stitch
pageChangeStitch.body: Use this when you want to replace a selected stitch segment with another stitch.

items.pickStitch: Pick a stitch from the list and watch the preview update
items.positionOptional: Some stitches support a position (e.g. X/T/F/E)
items.bundlePattern: When selecting a pattern/bundle, the preview shows nested structure
items.goParent: Use Go Parent to jump back up one level

pageAddToBundle.title: Add to bundle
pageAddToBundle.body: Use bundles for multiple stitches in the same spot (e.g. increases, popcorn).

items.switchTab: Switch to the Add to bundle tab
items.addSeveral: Add multiple stitches; they will be grouped into one bundle
items.countMultiplier: Use the × count to repeat a pattern multiple times
items.useForShaping: Great for shaping: increases/decreases and special clusters

pageBasicStitches.title: Basic stitches
pageBasicStitches.body: The common foundation stitches: single/half-double/double/treble (symbols: X/T/F/E).

pageDecorativeStitches.title: Decorative / utility
pageDecorativeStitches.body: Small utility stitches used for joins, turning, spacing, and shaping (may not increase stitch count).

pageIncrease.title: Increase
pageIncrease.body: An increase makes 2 stitches into the same spot (stitch count goes up).

items.v: Common symbols: V / TV / FV / EV (consume 1 → generate 2)
items.colorHint: Use the Total color hints to validate shaping
items.useWithPosition: Can also be combined with FL/BL/FP/BP when needed

pageDecrease.title: Decrease
pageDecrease.body: A decrease combines 2 stitches into 1 (stitch count goes down).

items.panel: Use the Decrease button on the right after selecting X/T/F/E
items.bpfa: If BP (back post) is selected with F-decrease, it displays as BPFA

pageCustom.title: Custom
pageCustom.body: When built-in stitches aren't enough, create a custom bundle and save it as a button.

items.when: Good for popcorn, shells, and special stitch groups
items.consumeGenerate: Set consume/generate so Total calculation stays correct
items.name: Give it a clear name so you can find it later

pageWorkflowTips.title: Workflow tips
pageWorkflowTips.body: Suggested approach: draft all rows first, then fine-tune row by row.

items.oneRowAtATime: Edit one row at a time to avoid confusion
items.checkTotal: Use Total color hints to validate shaping
items.adjustEarlier: If a row looks off, re-check the previous rows
items.okToSubmit: Colors are hints only: you can still submit the design

pageAddBundle.title: [Add a stitch group]
pageAddBundle.body:
Tap the buttons on the left to generate stitches.
Need more stitches? Tap again.
After saving, stitches are auto-formatted into a readable counted form.
You can also adjust the input count directly while typing stitches.

pageDeleteBundle.title: [Delete a stitch group]
pageDeleteBundle.body:
Open the row you want to edit.
Tap into the nested levels step by step.
Select the part you want to remove, then tap Delete (bottom-left).
Don’t forget to save!

pageIncrease.title: [Increase]
pageIncrease.body:
An increase is treated as: multiple stitches into the same stitch.
First, enter the base stitch.
Tap into that stitch (its count will be 1).
Use the top switch to choose Increase.
Add the same stitch again.
After saving, it auto-converts to the increase symbol.
You can also mix different stitches.
After going back up one level, you can still adjust the increase count.

pageDecrease.title: [Decrease]
pageDecrease.body:
Tap the Decrease button first, then pick a stitch in the middle list to enter a decrease.
Picked the wrong order? It’s ok—enter the single stitch first, then apply decrease inside.

pagePosition.title: [Stitch positions]
pagePosition.body:
Tap a position button on the right first, then tap a stitch on the left to enter a stitch with position.
You can also combine it with Decrease to enter longer stitch symbols.

pageCustom.title: [Custom stitch]
pageCustom.body:
Can’t enter the stitch you need?
Tap the “Custom” button.
Enter the custom stitch name, description, consume stitches, and generate stitches.
Save after filling in the fields.
Then you can use this stitch anywhere in the project!

pageAddRow.title: [Add a row]
pageAddRow.body:
At the bottom of the table, tap the plus button (small text).
Note: if the previous row has no stitches yet, you can’t add a new row below it.

pageAddFromOldRow.title: [Add from an existing row]
pageAddFromOldRow.body:
Tap a completed row.
Tap “Copy to latest row”.

pageRepeatMultipleRows.title: [Repeat multiple rows]
pageRepeatMultipleRows.body:
Tap the row you want to repeat.
Slide to choose how many rows.
Small text on screen will indicate this row needs repeating.

pageMoveRow.title: [Move this row]
pageMoveRow.body:
Tap the row you want to move.
Tap the move buttons to place it where you want.

pageDeleteRow.title: [Delete this row]
pageDeleteRow.body:
Tap the row you want to delete.
Tap the delete button (bottom-left).

pageMultiRowActions.title: [Apply actions to multiple rows]
pageMultiRowActions.body:
All the above features can be applied to multiple rows at once!
Tap “Select multiple rows” (top-right).
Select the first and last row numbers in the table.
Confirm the displayed range is correct.
Tap the action you want to run.
Remember to save!

page1.title: Recording (1/10)
page1.body: Tap the bottom-left “Start” button to begin recording your crochet time.

page2.title: Recording (2/10)
page2.body: Tap “Edit” to change the current status.

page3.title: Recording (3/10)
page3.body: Besides the default statuses, you can also add custom statuses.

page4.title: Recording (4/10)
page4.body: Each status can have a note—for example what you’re watching or where you’re headed.

page5.title: Recording (5/10)
page5.body: Before ending a session, don’t forget to save your current progress.

page6.title: Recording (6/10)
page6.body: Tap your current position in the table. If it’s a repeated stitch group, slide to choose which repetition you’re on.

page7.title: Recording (7/10)
page7.body: Tap an empty cell (no stitch) to quickly mark the whole row as completed.

page8.title: Recording (8/10)
page8.body: Tap the “Complete” button next to “Pause” to mark the whole component as completed.

page9.title: Recording (9/10)
page9.body: After everything is done, you can save a photo and any notes you want.

pageIntro.title: Crochet table
pageIntro.body: The crochet table is the soul of a design. Use Help often to get up to speed quickly!

pageRowNumber.title: [Row #]
pageRowNumber.body:
Which row of the fabric this is.
Tap it to edit row details.

pageStitch.title: [Stitch]
pageStitch.body:
The stitch pattern for each row.
Tap it to input the stitch pattern for that row.

pageTotalStitches.title: [Total stitches]
pageTotalStitches.body:
Automatically calculated from the Stitch column. It checks whether the previous row’s stitch count matches this row’s expected input.

Yellow: too few stitches
Green: correct
Red: too many stitches

special_same_project_5_in_day.name: Same Project Sprint
special_same_project_5_in_day.description: Complete 5 records of the same project in one day.

special_start_record_with_10_ongoing.name: Juggler
special_start_record_with_10_ongoing.description: Start a new record when you already have 10 ongoing records.

syncProject.title: Update design?
syncProject.message:
Updating the design will change the current pattern. If the update is before a stitch marker, it may cause the recorded position to become incorrect.

Are you sure you want to update this record? This action cannot be undone.

finishComponent.message: Mark this component as completed and move to the next one?

finishRecord.title: Complete this record?
finishRecord.message: All components are completed. Mark this entire record as completed?

editResultAfterFinishRecord.title: Add photos or thoughts?
editResultAfterFinishRecord.message: Do you want to update the photos or write any thoughts about your piece?

restartRecord.message: This will mark the record as not completed and reset all components to unfinished. Continue?
